element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from 24 teams with complete rosters
Exhibition single-game or playoff (single-elimination or best-of-7)
Player learns passing, shooting, positioning during gameplay
Strategic rotation of player lines (Genesis version saveable via EEPROM, SNES via passwords)
Players can sustain injuries affecting team composition mid-game

typical_tension_triggers: Opponent has puck in offensive zone
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based episodic pacing with natural tension/relief cycles per period; playoff mode creates escalating pressure across best-of-7 series

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible; reviewers noted 'scoring goals is still too easy' and criticized lack of challenging AI
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Consistent competitive play with strategic depth in line management and team selection
difficulty_curve.late_game: Playoff mode escalates stakes with best-of-7 series format providing cumulative pressure
notable_spikes: Transition to playoff mode increases match importance
Playing against stronger NHL teams with deeper rosters

notable_level_patterns.description: Single standalone game between any two teams; no progression or consequences; used for practice or casual play
Elimination-based progression; single-elimination or best-of-7 series format; winner receives Stanley Cup-like trophy; state persists across matches
